message:
So last spring I got a weather alert for a potential ice storm and thought, *nah, I've got this.* Spoiler: I did not have this. Found myself at 10 p.m. frantically searching my garage for flashlights I was *sure* I'd bought, while my partner was melting ice cubes in a bowl because we had no idea where the manual can opener was. (Apparently we've had an electric opener for five years.) The kicker? We found three half-dead flashlights and a working can opener... in the *back* of the pantry behind some expired applesauce.

Now I laugh about it, but mostly I just keep a tiny list in my phone of the basics we actually use — nothing fancy, just stuff that takes 20 minutes to think through when the sun is shining. Turns out a little calm prep means way less scrambling when things get real.

Have you got a "I should've been ready" story that made you finally take action?
